> **摘要**: 月球 GNSS 接收器实验 (LuGRE) 证明了在月球距离上可行的机载 GPS 和伽利略信号接收和跟踪，利用全球导航卫星系统 (GNSS) 来提高航天器定轨自主性已获得新的动力。这项工作处理 LuGRE 接收器在顺月空间和月球表面收集的同相和正交 (IQ) 快照，以评估多频率、多星座信号的可用性。来自 GPS 和伽利略之外的其他系统（包括 RNSS 和 SBAS 星座）的信号可以在记录的 IQ 快照中单独观测并成功获取。这些观测结果提供了第一个实验证据，证明来自多个星座的信号，包括不受 LuGRE 实时操作支持的系统，可以在距地球前所未有的距离处检测到。尽管具有最小采样率、4 位量化和短持续时间（200 ms-2 s），但通过补偿代码多普勒的混合相干/非相干采集级，可以从 IQ 快照中提取有用的可观测值。这些观察结果被用来调整模拟工具并执行扩展的模拟活动，表明纳入额外的星座可显着提高可用性；对于 26 dB-Hz 捕获阈值，具有至少四颗可见卫星的历元比例从总历元计数的 11% 增加到 46%。这些发现表明，北斗、RNSS 和 SBAS 信号可以显着增强基于 GNSS 的月球和地月任务自主性。
> **Abstract**: The use of Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) to increase spacecraft autonomy for orbit determination has gained renewed momentum following the Lunar GNSS Receiver Experiment (LuGRE), which demonstrated feasible onboard GPS and Galileo signal reception and tracking at lunar distances. This work processes in-phase and quadrature (IQ) snapshots collected by the LuGRE receiver in cis-lunar space and on the lunar surface to assess multi-frequency, multi-constellation signal availability. Signals from additional systems beyond GPS and Galileo, including RNSS and SBAS constellations, are observable and successfully acquired exclusively in the recorded IQ snapshots. These observations provide the first experimental evidence that signals from multiple constellations, including systems not supported by LuGRE realtime operations, are detectable at unprecedented distances from Earth. Useful observables can be extracted from the IQ snapshots, despite minimal sampling rates, 4-bit quantization, and short durations (200 ms-2 s), through a hybrid coherent/non-coherent acquisition stage compensating for code Doppler. These observations are exploited to tune simulation tools and to perform extended simulation campaigns, showing that the inclusion of additional constellations significantly improves availability; for a 26 dB-Hz acquisition threshold, the fraction of epochs with at least four visible satellites increases from 11% to 46% of the total epoch count. These findings indicate that BeiDou, RNSS, and SBAS signals can substantially enhance GNSS-based autonomy for lunar and cislunar missions.

## 神经元和认知(q-bio.NC:Neurons and Cognition)

【1】Gamma2Patterns: Deep Cognitive Attention Region Identification and Gamma-Alpha Pattern Analysis
- **标题**: Gamma2Patterns：深度认知注意力区域识别和 Gamma-Alpha 模式分析
- **链接**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.06257
> **作者**: Sobhana Jahan,Saydul Akbar Murad,Nick Rahimi,Noorbakhsh Amiri Golilarz
> **摘要**: 深度认知注意力的特点是伽马振荡增强和视觉行为协调。尽管这些机制具有生理重要性，但计算研究很少综合这些模式或识别对持续注意力最负责的神经区域。为了解决这一差距，这项工作引入了 Gamma2Patterns，这是一种多模式框架，通过利用互补的 Gamma 和 Alpha 波段脑电图活动以及眼动追踪测量来表征深度认知注意力。使用 SEED-IV 数据集 [1]，我们提取了 62 个通道或电极的光谱功率、基于突发的时间动态和注视扫视瞳孔信号，以分析高焦点（Gamma 主导）和低焦点（Alpha 主导）状态之间神经激活的差异。我们的研究结果表明，额极区、颞叶区、额前区和顶枕区表现出最强的伽马功率和爆发率，表明它们在深度注意力投入中发挥主导作用，而眼动追踪信号则证实了额叶区、额极区和额颞区的互补贡献。此外，我们还表明，与单独的 Alpha 功率相比，Gamma 功率和突发持续时间提供了更具辨别力的深度焦点标记，证明了它们对于注意力解码的价值。总的来说，这些结果建立了一个多模式、基于证据的皮质区域图和深度聚焦基础的振荡特征图，为人工智能系统中未来受大脑启发的注意力机制提供了神经生理学基础。
> **Abstract**: Deep cognitive attention is characterized by heightened gamma oscillations and coordinated visual behavior. Despite the physiological importance of these mechanisms, computational studies rarely synthesize these modalities or identify the neural regions most responsible for sustained focus. To address this gap, this work introduces Gamma2Patterns, a multimodal framework that characterizes deep cognitive attention by leveraging complementary Gamma and Alpha band EEG activity alongside Eye-tracking measurements. Using the SEED-IV dataset [1], we extract spectral power, burst-based temporal dynamics, and fixation-saccade-pupil signals across 62 channels or electrodes to analyze how neural activation differs between high-focus (Gamma-dominant) and low-focus (Alpha-dominant) states. Our findings reveal that frontopolar, temporal, anterior frontal, and parieto-occipital regions exhibit the strongest Gamma power and burst rates, indicating their dominant role in deep attentional engagement, while Eye-tracking signals confirm complementary contributions from frontal, frontopolar, and frontotemporal regions. Furthermore, we show that Gamma power and burst duration provide more discriminative markers of deep focus than Alpha power alone, demonstrating their value for attention decoding. Collectively, these results establish a multimodal, evidence-based map of cortical regions and oscillatory signatures underlying deep focus, providing a neurophysiological foundation for future brain-inspired attention mechanisms in AI systems.
